lmp:Semantics.lmp:Comment: Involved in oxygen transport from the lung to the various peripheral tissues
LVV-hemorphin-7 potentiates the activity of bradykinin, causing a decrease in blood pressure

Functions as an endogenous inhibitor of enkephalin-degrading enzymes such as DPP3, and as a selective antagonist of the P2RX3 receptor which is involved in pain signaling, these properties implicate it as a regulator of pain and inflammation

Heterotetramer of two alpha chains and two beta chains in adult hemoglobin A (HbA). Heterotetramer of two zeta chains and two beta chains in hemoglobin Portland-2, detected in fetuses and neonates with homozygous alpha-thalassemia

Glucose reacts non-enzymatically with the N-terminus of the beta chain to form a stable ketoamine linkage. This takes place slowly and continuously throughout the 120-day life span of the red blood cell. The rate of glycation is increased in patients with diabetes mellitus
S-nitrosylated; a nitric oxide group is first bound to Fe(2+) and then transferred to Cys-94 to allow capture of O(2)
Acetylated on Lys-60, Lys-83 and Lys-145 upon aspirin exposure

Genetic variations in HBB are involved in resistance to malaria [MIM:611162]. Hemoglobin S (Hb S), which at homozygosity is responsible for sickle cell anemia, is not associated with any clinical abnormality when heterozygous. At heterozygosity, Hb S confers an increase in protection from life-threatening malaria. Additional variants conferring resistance against severe malaria are hemoglobin C (Hb C) and hemoglobin E (Hb E)
One molecule of 2,3-bisphosphoglycerate can bind to two beta chains per hemoglobin tetramer
The modification form of Leu-142 is subject of controversy and could be the artifactual result of sample handling

It is unclear if hemoglobin Beckman (Hb Beckman) is defined by p.Ala136Glu or p.Ala136Asp. Hb Beckman has been originally identified by reverse phase-HPLC and tandem mass spectrometry, and has been reported as variant p.Ala136Glu (Ref.140). Subsequently, variant p.Ala136Asp has been reported based on HBB gene complete sequencing results (PubMed:19453576). Variant p.Ala136Asp has also been detected by mass spectrometry (PubMed:26209877). Although the name Hb Beckman is currently used for variant p.Ala136Asp, it cannot be ruled out that Hb Beckman is indeed variant p.Ala136Glu (PubMed:19453576)

lmp:Reference.lmp:Citation: Nucleotide sequence analysis of coding and noncoding regions of human beta-globin mRNA.
The nucleotide sequence of the human beta-globin gene.
The beta-globin recombinational hotspot reduces the effects of strong selection around HbC, a recently arisen mutation providing resistance to malaria.
DNA sequence of the human beta-globin gene isolated from a healthy Chinese.
Unexpected patterns of globin mutations in thalassemia patients from north of Portugal.
Rapid detection of electrophoretically silent, unstable human hemoglobin 'Louisville', (Beta; Phe 42 Leu/TTT to CTT) by cDNA sequencing of mRNA.
Electrophoretically silent, very unstable, thalassemic mutation at codon 114 of beta globin (hemoglobin Durham-N.C.) detected by cDNA sequencing of mRNA, from a Russian women.
A rare beta chain variant 'Hemoglobin Ty Gard:Pro 124 Gln' found in a Caucasian female with erythrocytosis.
Heterozygote C->A beta-thalassemia mutation at the intron-2/848 nucleotide of beta globin gene was detected on a Northern European (Caucasian) individual.
A new hemoglobin, beta chain variant 'Hb S-Wake' confirmed to be on the same chromosome with hemoglobin S mutation, detected in an African-American family.
Coexistence of the hemoglobin O-Arab (Glu 121 Lys) and beta-thalassemia (intron-2; nucleotide 745 C->G) was detected in a Turkish patient.
Thalassaemic trait cause by C-T substitution at position -90 in proximal CACCC box of beta-globin gene in China family.
The differently expressed genes in the lymphocyte of recovered SARS patients.
Beta-thalassemia G->C mutation at the nucleotide 5 position of intron 1 of beta globin gene was detected in Asian-Indian female with two polymorphisms in cis.
Hb Dothan: a novel beta chain variant due to (-GTG) deletion between the codons 25/26 of beta globin gene detected in a Caucasian male baby.
Complete sequencing and characterization of 21,243 full-length human cDNAs.
Cloning of human full open reading frames in Gateway(TM) system entry vector (pDONR201).
The status, quality, and expansion of the NIH full-length cDNA project: the Mammalian Gene Collection (MGC).
The constitution of normal adult human haemoglobin.
Gene mutations in human haemoglobin: the chemical difference between normal and sickle cell haemoglobin.
Human basal tear peptidome characterization by CID, HCD, and ETD followed by in silico and in vitro analyses for antimicrobial peptide identification.
Isolation of a hemoglobin-derived opioid peptide from cerebrospinal fluid of patients with cerebrovascular bleedings.
Globin chain synthesis in hemolytic anemia reticulocytes. A case of hemoglobin Burke.
Cloning specific complete polyadenylylated 3'-terminal cDNA segments.
X-ray diffraction study of binding of 2,3-diphosphoglycerate to human deoxyhaemoglobin.
Sites of acetylation of sickle cell hemoglobin by aspirin.
The glycosylation of hemoglobin: relevance to diabetes mellitus.
Sites of nonenzymatic glycosylation of human hemoglobin A.
Molecular analysis of the beta-thalassemia phenotype associated with inheritance of hemoglobin E (alpha 2 beta2(26)Glu leads to Lys).
Human hemoglobin Portland II (zeta 2 beta 2). Isolation and characterization of Portland hemoglobin components and their constituent globin chains.
Haemoglobin binding with haptoglobin. Localization of the haptoglobin-binding sites on the beta-chain of human haemoglobin by synthetic overlapping peptides encompassing the entire chain.
Beta 141 Leu is not deleted in the unstable haemoglobin Atlanta-Coventry but is replaced by a novel amino acid of mass 129 daltons.
S-nitrosohaemoglobin: a dynamic activity of blood involved in vascular control.
Crystal structure of the S-nitroso form of liganded human hemoglobin.
Ancient origins of nitric oxide signaling in biological systems.
Hemoglobin C associated with protection from severe malaria in the Dogon of Mali, a West African population with a low prevalence of hemoglobin S.
The role of beta chains in the control of the hemoglobin oxygen binding function: chimeric human/mouse proteins, structure, and function.
Hemoglobin E: a balanced polymorphism protective against high parasitemias and thus severe P falciparum malaria.
Spinorphin as an endogenous inhibitor of enkephalin-degrading enzymes: roles in pain and inflammation.
Hookworm aspartic protease, Na-APR-2, cleaves human hemoglobin and serum proteins in a host-specific fashion.
How malaria has affected the human genome and what human genetics can teach us about malaria.
Hemorphin and hemorphin-like peptides isolated from dog pancreas and sheep brain are able to potentiate bradykinin activity in vivo.
Structure-activity relationship studies of spinorphin as a potent and selective human P2X(3) receptor antagonist.
Initial characterization of the human central proteome.
An enzyme assisted RP-RPLC approach for in-depth analysis of human liver phosphoproteome.
Structure of sickled erythrocytes and of sickle-cell hemoglobin fibers.
Crystal structure of sickle-cell deoxyhemoglobin at 5 A resolution.
Three-dimensional Fourier synthesis of human deoxyhaemoglobin at 2.5-A resolution: refinement of the atomic model.
The structure of human carbonmonoxy haemoglobin at 2.7-A resolution.
The crystal structure of human deoxyhaemoglobin at 1.74 A resolution.
High-resolution X-ray study of deoxyhemoglobin Rothschild 37 beta Trp-->Arg: a mutation that creates an intersubunit chloride-binding site.
Structure-function relationships in the low-affinity mutant haemoglobin Aalborg (Gly74 (E18)beta-->Arg).
Human deoxyhaemoglobin-2,3-diphosphoglycerate complex low-salt structure at 2.5 A resolution.
Crystal structure of T state haemoglobin with oxygen bound at all four haems.
High-resolution crystal structures of human hemoglobin with mutations at tryptophan 37beta: structural basis for a high-affinity T-state.
Crystal structure of deoxy-human hemoglobin beta6 Glu-->Trp. Implications for the structure and formation of the sickle cell fiber.
Structure of mutant human carbonmonoxyhemoglobin C (betaE6K) at 2.0 A resolution.
Structure of fully liganded Hb zeta2beta2s trapped in a tense conformation.
Hemoglobin Freiburg: abnormal hemoglobin due to deletion of a single amino acid residue.
Two new hemoglobins. Hemoglobin Alabama (beta39(C5)Gln leads to Lys) and hemoglobin Montgomery (alpha 48(CD 6) Leu leads to Arg).
Functional and physicochemical studies of hemoglobin St. Louis beta 28 (B10) Leu replaced by Gln: a variant with ferric beta heme iron.
Molecular basis for dominantly inherited inclusion body beta-thalassemia.
Hb Alesha or alpha 2 beta (2)67(E11)Val-->Met: a new unstable hemoglobin variant identified through sequencing of amplified DNA.
Hemoglobin J Altgeld Gardens. A hemoglobin variant with a substitution of the proximal histidine of the beta-chain.
A new haemoglobin J from Turkey -- Hb Ankara (beta10 (A7) Ala-Asp).
Hb J-Antakya or alpha 2 beta (2)65(E9)Lys-->Met in a Turkish family and Hb complutense or alpha 2 beta (2)127(H5)Gln-->Glu in a Spanish family; correction of a previously published identification.
A new unstable and low oxygen affinity hemoglobin variant: Hb J-Auckland [beta 25(B7)Gly-->Asp].
Identification of a new high oxygen affinity hemoglobin variant: Hb Aurora [beta 139(H17) Asn-->Tyr].
Hemoglobin Brest [beta 127 (H5)Gln-->Lys] a new unstable human hemoglobin variant located at the alpha 1 beta 1 interface with specific electrophoretic behavior.
Hemoglobin Brisbane: beta68 Leu replaced by His. A new high oxygen affinity variant.
A new hemoglobin with high oxygen affinity -- hemoglobin Bunbury: alpha 2 beta 2 [94 (FG1) Asp replaced by Asn].
Hemoglobin J Cairo: beta 65 (E9) Lys leads to Gln, A new hemoglobin variant discovered in an Egyptian family.
A new haemoglobin variant, haemoglobin Camperdown (beta 104 (G6) arginine->serine).
Haemoglobin caribbean beta91 (F7) Leu replaced by Arg: a mildly haemoglobin with a low oxygen affinity.
A silent hemoglobin variant detected by HPLC: hemoglobin City of Hope beta 69 (E13) Gly-->Ser.
Hb Coimbra or alpha 2 beta (2)99(G1)Asp-->Glu, a newly discovered high oxygen affinity variant.
Hb Costa Rica or alpha 2 beta 2 77(EF1)His-->Arg: the first example of a somatic cell mutation in a globin gene.
Hemoglobin Debrousse (beta 96[FG3]Leu-->Pro): a new unstable hemoglobin with twofold increased oxygen affinity.
Hemoglobin Dhonburi alpha 2 beta 2 126 (H4) Val-->Gly: a new unstable beta variant producing a beta-thalassemia intermedia phenotype in association with beta zero-thalassemia.
Two new human hemoglobin variants caused by unusual mutational events: Hb Zaire contains a five residue repetition within the alpha-chain and Hb Duino has two residues substituted in the beta-chain.
A novel beta-globin structural mutant, Hb Brescia (beta 114 Leu-Pro), causing a severe beta-thalassemia intermedia phenotype.
A novel beta-globin mutation, beta Durham-NC [beta 114 Leu-->Pro], produces a dominant thalassemia-like phenotype.
Hb J-Europa [beta 62(E6)Ala-->Asp]: normal oxygen binding properties in a new variant involving a residue located distal to the heme.
Hb Geelong [beta 139(H17)Asn-->Asp].
Hemoglobin Grange-Blanche [beta 27(B9) Ala-->Val], a new variant with normal expression and increased affinity for oxygen.
Hb Graz or alpha 2 beta 2(2)(NA2)His-->Leu; a new beta chain variant observed in four families from southern Austria.
Hb Helsinki: a variant with a high oxygen affinity and a substitution at a 2,3-DPG binding site (beta82[EF6] Lys replaced by Met).
Hb Himeji or beta 140 (H18) Ala-->Asp. A slightly unstable hemoglobin with increased beta N-terminal glycation.
Hb Hinsdale [beta 139 (H17)Asn-->Lys]: a variant in the central cavity showing reduced affinity for oxygen and 2,3-diphosphoglycerate.
HB Hinwil or beta 38(C4)Thr-->Asn: a new beta chain variant detected in a Swiss family.
Hb Howick [beta 37(C3)Trp-->Gly]: a new high oxygen affinity variant of the alpha 1 beta 2 contact.
The structure of hemoglobin Indianapolis [beta112(G14) arginine]. An unstable variant detectable only by isotopic labeling.
Hb Isehara (or Hb Redondo) [beta 92 (F8) His-->Asn]: an unstable variant with a proximal histidine substitution at the heme contact.
Hemoglobin Istanbul: substitution of glutamine for histidine in a proximal histidine (F8(92)).
Hb Jacksonville [alpha 2 beta 2(54)(D5)Val-->Asp]: a new unstable variant found in a patient with hemolytic anemia.
Hemoglobin Jianghua [beta 120(GH3) Lys leads to Ile]: a new fast-moving variant found in China.
Hb Karlskoga or alpha 2 beta (2)21(B3) Asp-->His: a new slow-moving variant found in Sweden.
Structural study of hemoglobin Knossos, beta 27 (B9) Ala leads to Ser. A new abnormal hemoglobin present as a silent beta-thalassemia.
Hb Kodaira [beta 146(HC3)His-->Gln]: a new beta chain variant with an amino acid substitution at the C-terminus.
A new electrophoretically-silent hemoglobin variant: hemoglobin Kofu or alpha 2 beta 2 84 (EF8) Thr-->Ile.
Hb Hradec Kralove (Hb HK) or alpha 2 beta 2 115(G17)Ala-->Asp, a severely unstable hemoglobin variant resulting in a dominant beta-thalassemia trait in a Czech family.
Hemoglobin La Desirade alpha A2 beta 2 129 (H7) Ala-->Val: a new unstable hemoglobin.
Structure of the EF corner favors deamidation of asparaginyl residues in hemoglobin: the example of Hb La Roche-sur-Yon [beta 81 (EF5) Leu-->His].
Hb Las Palmas or alpha 2 beta 2(49)(CD8)Ser-->Phe, a mildly unstable hemoglobin variant.
Hb Linkoping (beta 36 Pro-->Thr): a new high oxygen affinity hemoglobin variant found in two families of Finnish origin.
Hemoglobin Maputo: a new beta-chain variant (alpha 2 beta 2 47 (CD6) Asp replaced by Tyr) in combination with hemoglobin S, identified by high performance liquid chromatography (HPLC).
Hb Matera [beta 55(D6)Met-->Lys]: a new unstable hemoglobin variant in an Italian family.
Hemoglobin Miyashiro (beta 23[B5] val substituting for gly) an electrophoretically silent variant discovered by the isopropanol test.
Hemoglobin Mizuho or beta 68 (E 12) leucine leads to proline, a new unstable variant associated with severe hemolytic anemia.
A new variant, HB Muscat [alpha 2 beta (2)32(B14)Leu-->Val] observed in association with HB S in an Arabian family.
Hb N-Timone [alpha 2 beta 2(8)(A5)Lys-->Glu]: a new fast-moving variant with normal stability and oxygen affinity.
A new unstable, high oxygen affinity hemoglobin: Hb Nagoya or beta 97 (FG4) His-->Pro.
Hb D-Neath or beta 121 (GH4) Glu-->Ala: a new member of the Hb D family.
Hemoglobin North Chicago (beta 36 [C2] proline-->serine): a new high affinity hemoglobin.
Haemoglobin North Shore-Caracas beta 134 (H12) valine replaced by glutamic acid.
Hb Olomouc or alpha 2 beta 2(86)(F2)Ala-->Asp, a new high oxygen affinity variant.
Hemoglobin Palmerston North beta 23 (B5) Val replaced by Phe. A new variant identified in a patient with polycythemia.
Hemoglobin Pierre-Benite [beta 90(F6)Glu-->Asp], a new high affinity variant found in a French family.
Hemoglobin Presbyterian: beta108 (G10) asparagine leads to lysine, A hemoglobin variant with low oxygen affinity.
Germline mosaicism for an alanine to valine substitution at residue beta 140 in hemoglobin Puttelange, a new variant with high oxygen affinity.
Hemoglobin Quin-Hai, beta 78 (EF2) Leu replaced by Arg, a new abnormal hemoglobin found in Guangdong, China.
Hemoglobin Rambam (beta69[E13]Gly-->Asp), a pitfall in the assessment of diabetic control: characterization by electrospray mass spectrometry and HPLC.
Hemoglobin Randwick or beta 15 (A12)Trp-->Gly: a new unstable beta-chain hemoglobin variant.
Hemoglobin Rio Grande [beta 8 (A5) Lys leads to Thr] a new variant found in a Mexican-American family.
Hemoglobin Rush (beta 101 (g3) glutamine): a new unstable hemoglobin causing mild hemolytic anemia.
Hemoglobin Saitama or beta 117 (G19) His leads to Pro, a new variant causing hemolytic disease.
Chemical studies of several varieties of Hb M.
Hemoglobin Shelby [beta 131(H9) Gln-->Lys] a correction to the structure of hemoglobin Deaconess and hemoglobin Leslie.
Hb J Sicilia: beta 65 (E9) Lys-Asn, a beta homologue of Hb Zambia.
A new unstable and low oxygen affinity hemoglobin variant: Hb Stanmore [beta 111(G13)Val-->Ala].
Hemoglobin Saint Mande beta 102 (G4) asn replaced by tyr: a new low oxygen affinity variant.
Hemoglobin Windsor or beta 11 (A8)Val-->Asp: a new unstable beta-chain hemoglobin variant producing a hemolytic anemia.
A new abnormal variant, Hb Yahata or beta 112(G14)Cys-->Tyr, found in a Japanese: structural confirmation by DNA sequencing of the beta-globin gene.
A new unstable hemoglobin, Hb Yokohama beta 31 (B13)Leu substituting for Pro, causing hemolytic anemia.
Hemoglobin Hammersmith (beta 42 (CD1) Phe replaced by Ser) associated with severe hemolytic anemia.
Hb Bruxelles: alpha 2A beta (2)41 or 42(C7 or CD1)Phe deleted.
Hb Zengcheng or alpha 2 beta(2)114(G16)Leu-->Met.
Hb Beckman alpha135 (H13) ala-to-glu: a new unstable variant and reduced oxygen affinity.
Haemoglobin Manukau beta 67[E11] Val-->Gly: transfusion-dependent haemolytic anaemia ameliorated by coexisting alpha thalassaemia.
A novel silent posttranslational mechanism converts methionine to aspartate in hemoglobin Bristol (beta 67[E11] Val-Met->Asp).
Hb Iraq-Halabja beta10 (A7) Ala-->Val (GCC-->GTC): a new beta-chain silent variant in a family with multiple Hb disorders.
Identification of Hb Villejuif [beta123(H1)Thr-->Ile] in Southern Italy.
Hb Tsukumi [beta117(G19)His-->Tyr] found in a Moroccan woman.
Hb Canterbury [beta112(G14)Cys-->Phe]: a new, mildly unstable variant.
Double heterozygosity for Hb Pyrgos [beta83(EF7)Gly-->Asp] and Hb E [beta26(B8)Glu-->Lys] found in association with alpha-thalassemia.
Hb Santander [beta34(B16)Val-->Asp (GTC-->GAC)]: a new unstable variant found as a de novo mutation in a Spanish patient.
Two new hemoglobin variants with increased oxygen affinity: Hb Nantes [beta34(B16)Val-->Leu] and Hb Vexin [beta116(G18)His-->Leu].
The 'hot-spot' of Hb E [beta26(B8)Glu-->Lys] in Southeast Asia: beta-globin anomalies in the Lao Theung population of southern Laos.
A novel hemoglobin variant beta135(H13) Ala > Asp identified in an asymptomatic Korean family by direct sequencing: suggesting a new insight into Hb Beckman mutation.
Mass spectrometry based characterization of Hb Beckman variant in a falsely elevated HbA(1c) sample.
Low affinity hemoglobinopathy (Hb Vigo) due to a new mutation of beta globin gene (c200 A>T; Lys>Ile). A cause of rare anemia misdiagnosis.
